jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Executive Director – Yolo Healthy Aging Alliance (YHAA)**

Yolo Healthy Aging Alliance (YHAA) seeks a dynamic Executive Director to lead strategic, operational, and fundraising efforts that advance its mission to improve the well\-being of older adults in Yolo County. This role partners closely with the Board of Directors to expand programs, strengthen partnerships, and enhance advocacy, education, and service delivery.

**Compensation \& Location**

* Full\-time, exempt, hybrid (Davis, CA)
* Salary: $80,000–$95,000 (DOE)
* Benefits: Flexible schedule, paid leave, partial health premium reimbursement
* Requires local travel within Yolo County (occasional Sacramento travel)

**About YHAA**

YHAA is a nonprofit dedicated to supporting older adults by:

* **Education:** Hosting workshops, events, and maintaining an informational website
* **Collaboration:** Convening 90\+ service providers to coordinate care and resources
* **Advocacy:** Influencing policy on healthcare, housing, transportation, and more
* **Programs:** Delivering services such as case management, transportation support, digital access, and home safety improvements

**Position Summary**

Reporting to the Board, the Executive Director oversees all organizational functions, including strategy, operations, fundraising, communications, staff leadership, and community engagement. The role serves as YHAA’s primary spokesperson and relationship builder with funders, partners, and the public.

**First\-Year Priorities**

* Implement the first year of YHAA's participation as a provider in the CalAIM program
* Build strong relationships with funders, agencies, and partners
* Expand fundraising and secure new revenue sources
* Strengthen communications and visibility
* Support and develop staff and internal systems

**Key Responsibilities**

**Fundraising \& Grants**

* Lead fundraising strategy and annual plan
* Write and manage grants; ensure compliance and reporting
* Cultivate donors and sponsorships
* Plan fundraising events with the Board

**Advocacy \& Policy**

* Lead policy initiatives and advocacy efforts
* Collaborate with partners on aging\-related legislation and programs

**Communications \& Community Engagement**

* Serve as public spokesperson
* Oversee events, website, and communications strategy
* Build partnerships with community organizations and officials

**Financial Management**

* Oversee budgeting, financial reporting, and compliance
* Monitor revenues, expenses, and contracts
* Ensure sound financial practices and audits

**Leadership \& Board Relations**

* Support and advise the Board
* Lead strategic and operational planning
* Engage stakeholders, volunteers, and partners
* Foster a diverse, collaborative organizational culture

**Operations \& HR**

* Supervise staff and programs
* Ensure legal and regulatory compliance (including HIPAA)
* Oversee technology, operations, and performance tracking

**Qualifications**

**Preferred Experience**

* Bachelor’s degree (advanced degree preferred; relevant fields include public health, gerontology, social work)
* 3\+ years in fundraising and grant management
* Leadership or management experience
* Strong communication, financial, and people management skills
* Knowledge of aging services, policy, and systems (e.g., Medicare/Medi\-Cal)

**Additional Strengths**

* Experience in nonprofit leadership or aging services
* Ability to build partnerships with government and community stakeholders
* Strong technical and organizational skills

**Other Requirements**

* Valid driver’s license or reliable transportation

**Ideal Candidate**

* Passionate about aging issues and equity
* Collaborative, strategic, and adaptable leader
* Strong relationship builder with integrity and professionalism
* Committed to diversity, inclusion, and community impact

**Application**

Submit resume and cover letter to **recruiting@yolohealthyaging.org**  
Priority deadline: **July 31, 2026** (open until filled)

For a more in\-depth job posting, please visit

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $95,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule
* Paid time off

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Davis, CA 95616

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**SUBMIT APPLICATION HERE: https://executivedirector.ju.mp/** 

**ABOUT BERKELEY HERBAL CENTER**  
For over 30 years, the Berkeley Herbal Center (BHC) has been a cornerstone of the Bay Area’s herbal medicine community — a place where rigorous clinical training, deep reverence for plant medicine, and a commitment to community health have grown together.

Rooted in Berkeley and shaped by the Bay Area’s spirit of innovation, diversity, and deep commitment to community wellbeing, BHC is a nonprofit school dedicated to cultivating the next generation of clinical herbalists. We recognize that our students descend from many rich and diverse herbal traditions, and we actively celebrate and integrate that diversity into everything we do.

BHC offers eight distinct certification programs, including our comprehensive Clinical Practicum. Our educational model weaves rigorous academic study with deep hands\-on clinical practice —preparing future practitioners with the knowledge, confidence, and ethical grounding to offer sustainable, accessible herbal medicine to their communities.

At the heart of BHC is our busy, low\-cost Community Clinic, where students work directly with clients under the close guidance of experienced clinical faculty. Beyond formal training, BHC fosters a living love for plants through short courses, public health days, and free community events.

**ABOUT THE ROLE**  
BHC is entering a pivotal chapter: our student body has grown and diversified, our certification programs are expanding, and the organization is building the infrastructure needed to serve students, faculty, and the greater community with excellence for decades to come. The Executive Director will be both a visionary and empathetic leader and a skilled manager — someone who can hold the academic and clinical integrity of our programs, support our students, and strengthen the operational, financial, and cultural foundations of the organization. Critically, this ED will lead a team, not carry the organization alone.

We are looking for someone who deeply understands clinical herbalism, can work towards creating a culture of care, has compassion and curiosity, and can speak credibly to students, faculty, and community partners about the trajectory of the field.

The Executive Director is a full\-time, on\-site position based at BHC’s Berkeley office. Regular in\-person presence is expected and essential to providing consistent leadership, visibility, and support to staff and students.

**YOUR LEADERSHIP TEAM**  
The ED will lead and be supported by the following roles:

* Executive Assistant — manages daily administrative operations, calendar, board support, and logistics.
* Academic Coordinator — owns curriculum delivery, faculty communication, and student\-facing documentation across all certification programs
* Retail Shop Manager — leads the retail department autonomously; reports to ED for strategic alignment
* Pharmacy Director — leads the dispensary autonomously; reports to ED for strategic alignment
* Clinic Director — oversees community clinic operations and clinical faculty

**DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES**

***Academic Program Integrity***

The ED holds vision and accountability for all certification programs. Day\-to\-day academic coordination is managed by the Academic Coordinator.

* Set and uphold academic standards and learning outcomes across all certification programs
* Hire, support, and evaluate the Academic Coordinator
* Recruit and retain qualified clinical herbalism instructors and teaching staff
* Maintain Community Guidelines and systems for student support and grievance resolution
* Ensure students receive clear, proactive communication about program requirements and expectations

***Operations \& Compliance***

The ED owns compliance fully — both strategy and execution. The Executive Assistant supports administrative operations.

* Conduct and maintain a thorough audit of all administrative and operating compliance gaps
* Maintain organized systems for employment records, legal filings, leases, and policy documentation
* Hire and support an Executive Assistant to manage daily administrative operations
* Maintain and update organizational policies; ensure they are followed in classrooms and clinical settings
* Implement tech onboarding and training for staff members

***Financial Sustainability \& Fundraising***  
The ED leads fundraising strategy and donor stewardship. The Board of Directors actively participates in donor relationships and fundraising activity.

* Develop and execute a fundraising strategy in partnership with the Board, including grants, individual donors, and community campaigns
* Cultivate and steward key donor and partner relationships
* Develop and manage the annual budget in partnership with the Board Treasurer
* Explore and develop new revenue streams in alignment with BHC’s mission
* Manage all leases and rental agreements with attention to cost\-containment

***Marketing, Communications \& Enrollment***

The ED owns marketing strategy and hands\-on execution. A Marketing Coordinator is a planned future hire; until that role is filled, the ED manages all marketing functions directly.

* Develop and execute a comprehensive marketing strategy to grow enrollment and community visibility
* Manage digital marketing channels directly: SEO, Google Ads, social media, and email campaigns
* Oversee a full website rebuild — vetting vendors, managing the project timeline, and ensuring the site reflects BHC’s brand and programs
* Establish and maintain a consistent brand voice across all platforms
* Monitor enrollment trends and adjust strategy accordingly
* Maintain proficiency in organizational tools including QuickBooks, G Suite, Google Classroom, Google Ads, Adobe Acrobat, Microsoft Office, Zoom, and LillySoft

***Retail Shop \& Pharmacy — Strategic Oversight Only***

The Retail Shop Manager and Pharmacy Director each lead their own departments. The ED provides executive overs

jobs.title: Executive Director - Nonprofit
jobs.description:
**Stanislaus Partners in Education (SPIE)** is seeking a full\-time Executive Director based in Modesto, CA. This position reports to the Board of Directors.

Stanislaus Partners in Education (SPIE) is a nonprofit intermediary that connects classroom learning with the regional economy. For more than 30 years, we have worked in the space between education and industry, allowing us to remain nimble, agile, and flexible. SPIE is committed to its foundational services: helping K–12 school district partners connect with local businesses that provide meaningful career\-connected learning opportunities for students. By aligning academic pathways with industry needs, we help students discover rewarding careers that support the Central Valley’s economic vitality.

**Key Responsibilities include:**

*Strategic Leadership \& Intermediary Focus*

* Provide strategic planning and leadership that keep organizational priorities focused for the Board, staff, and network partners.
* Serve as the key liaison between K–12 school district partners and industry leaders in agriculture, manufacturing, construction, health services, and emerging sectors.
* Champion the continued success and growth of core programs, including Career Education Grants, Educators in Industry, and daily Work\-Based Learning (WBL) connections led by Partnership Strategists.
* Lead efforts to expand organizational capacity and deliver comprehensive, high\-impact intermediary services to current and future district partners.
* Navigate school board, academic, corporate, and industrial settings to communicate the value and return on investment of partnerships to all stakeholders.
* Direct special projects using partner resources, board members, volunteers, or external consultants.

*Revenue Generation \& Financial Oversight*

* Lead efforts to grow SPIE’s annual operating budget through a dynamic financial plan and revenue model that meets the region’s increasing demand for career\-connected learning.
* Oversee revenue generation, budgeting, and identification of new funding sources, including district contracts and foundation grants.
* Engage and leverage the Board of Directors, including the Finance \& Funding Committee, to establish paid employer partnership models and create a new funding model for 2027 and beyond.
* Assess the true cost of all programs to ensure alignment with revenue and organizational health.

*Team Management \& Operational Excellence*

* Lead, manage, and develop a dedicated team that includes an Administrative Assistant, a Program Manager, and Partnership Strategists.
* Set annual goals, key performance indicators (KPIs), and documented internal processes, and hold staff accountable for results.
* Implement and manage a CRM system to track network growth, district relationships, and partnership touchpoints.
* Engage and manage outsourced consultants for specialized functions such as public relations, marketing, social media strategy, and grant writing.

**Minimum Qualifications Include**

* At least three years of professional leadership, management, or executive experience in a nonprofit environment.
* At least two years of experience leading fundraising, grant writing, corporate sponsorships, and budget oversight.
* Exceptional verbal, written, and public speaking skills, with the ability to serve as a compelling, highly visible community ambassador.
* Access to reliable transportation and availability to work varied shifts for community meetings and events, with or without reasonable accommodation.
* Experience working closely with a Board of Directors to support governance and fiscal responsibility.
* Proven ability to build relationships and unite diverse business, education, and community leaders around shared goals.
* A high\-energy builder who is flexible, responsive, adaptable, and creative, with the ability to build trust quickly and maintain strong community roots.

**Preferred Qualifications Include**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience.
* Two years of experience working in or partnering with the K–12 education system, with a strong understanding of the Central Valley’s economic landscape.

**Physical Demands \& Work Environment**

* This position requires working in a hybrid office environment, using standard office equipment, and traveling locally and varying times of the day and evening, to partner sites, schools, and industry facilities across Stanislaus County. Must be able to perform the essential functions of the position, with or without reasonable accommodation.

**Equal Opportunity Employer**

* Stanislaus Partners in Education is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all employees. Employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

Pay: $110,000\.00 \- $130,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Modesto, CA 95354

jobs.title: Managing Director
jobs.description:
### **About Us**



Orr Group is a strategic partner to nonprofit organizations, helping them solve some of the world's most pressing challenges. Through our embedded partnership model, we go beyond traditional consulting \- providing hands\-on leadership, expertise, and execution to drive transformative fundraising and organizational growth. For over 30 years, we have collaborated with mission\-driven organizations to design and implement innovative fundraising strategies, grow revenue, and build capacity to ensure long\-term success. Our work spans campaign management, major gifts strategy, board development, planned giving, talent management, and beyond. We remain nimble, forward\-thinking, and results\-driven, helping our clients navigate an ever\-evolving philanthropic landscape.



Orr Groups has offices based in Washington, DC, New York City, and Chicago, and will consider candidates nationwide. Washington, New York, and Chicago represent the firm's primary client relationships and greatest near\-term opportunities. Candidates in other major markets will be considered where there is a demonstrated existing network and documented market opportunity. The ideal candidate brings established senior relationships they can activate from day one and the flexibility to travel up to 25% to serve clients across geographies.


### **Position Summary**



Managing Directors at Orr Group are responsible for driving business development through sourcing, securing, growing, and retaining client relationships for the firm. They partner with the Growth Team to cultivate relationships that deploy Orr Group's services across the sector — serving as the firm's most senior external face and primary revenue drivers.



This is an entrepreneurial role for a self\-starter with a demonstrated ability to independently build and manage a significant book of business. The ideal candidate brings an established network of nonprofit leaders, a proven record of generating revenue through consultative relationship development, and the credibility to serve as a trusted advisor to CEOs, development executives, and boards. Experience as a Chief Development Officer or equivalent senior nonprofit leader, along with firsthand leadership of a major capital or comprehensive campaign, is strongly preferred.



Managing Directors manage a combination of regional and national clients. Depending on client needs, this role may require up to 25% travel or time working at a client site. Candidates based in Washington, DC or New York City are strongly preferred. Candidates in other major markets nationwide will be considered where there is a demonstrated network and documented market opportunity.


### **Responsibilities**



Managing Directors are expected to arrive with the relationships, credibility, expertise, and drive to build a robust client portfolio. While the Growth Team provides strategic partnership and support, new MDs should expect to develop their pipeline independently in the first year.



Responsibilities include:


* Own the full business development lifecycle from identifying prospects and cultivating relationships to negotiating and closing new client engagements.
* Independently build and manage a client pipeline through personal networks, targeted outreach, and market presence.
* Meet or exceed annual business development and revenue goals through new client acquisition and expansion of existing relationships.
* Secure and expand C\-suite and board\-level relationships across significant nonprofits, leveraging both self\-sourced opportunities and Growth Team support.
* Drive client retention through proactive relationship management, delivering measurable satisfaction and long\-term engagement.
* Become knowledgeable about all the firm's Practice Areas in order to identify prospective client needs and position appropriate services and expertise to address them.
* Identify and design the specific service or combination of services a client will engage Orr Group to provide.
* Maintain accountability for overall client profitability, satisfaction, and retention.
* Coordinate with and provide feedback to the Practice Area Leads to strengthen service offerings and identify emerging market opportunities.
* Build a personal brand as a thought leader and expert in one or more areas of Orr Group's targeted expertise, representing Orr Group through speaking engagements, professional associations, writing, and industry events.
* Champion innovative, efficient ways of working across the team, including the thoughtful use of AI to strengthen client service, sharpen strategic thinking, and improve how Orr Group delivers value.


Managing Directors may also serve as the leader on client projects to ensure high\-quality client service delivery. Generally, MDs provide executive oversight and management of the client relationship and ensure that services are delivered on time, in scope, and on budget.



Additional responsibilities include but are not limited to:


* Communicating effectively with a wide variety of audiences, including board members, C\-suite, staff, donors, and outside advisors.
* Providing high\-level oversight of client engagement teams, scopes, and service delivery.
* Serving as mentor and coach to team members.
* Presenting strategic recommendations and distilling complex analyses into actionable insights.
* Discovering and employing new and innovative solutions that create value for clients and strengthen Orr Group's competitive position.

### **Qualifications**


* Bachelor's degree required.
* 15\+ years of nonprofit sector experience, with significant exposure to fundraising strategy, executive leadership, or nonprofit consulting at a senior level.
* Prior CDO, VP of Development, or equivalent senior in\-house fundraising leadership strongly preferred.
* Demonstrated track record of independently sourcing and closing new business and consistently achieving revenue goals.
* Experience managing or lea

jobs.title: Executive Director - Fire Family Foundation
jobs.description:
**This is a remote position. Candidates must live or work on Pacific Time zone hours. Western US candidates highly preferred.**

  

Firefighters First Federal Credit Union has proudly been serving the firefighter community since 1935\. We have built trusting relationships within the fire family as the nation’s largest exclusive firefighter credit union. Our commitment to providing our members with exceptional financial products and services, along with our legendary service, has allowed us to grow our membership nationally. Join a team that values its employees, invests in professional growth, and makes an impact in the firefighter community.


Currently, we have an immediate opening for an **Executive Director**to lead our Fire Family Foundation! The Executive Director (ED) will serve as the head of the foundation dedicated to supporting firefighter families by addressing the emotional, psychological, and overall well\-being challenges they face due to the inherent stress of the firefighting profession. The ED will provide visionary leadership, strategic direction, and operational oversight to ensure the foundation fulfills its mission. The ideal candidate has a deep understanding of the psychological challenges in firefighter families with a passion for promoting mental health and wellness. Additionally, the ED will focus on developing relationships with stakeholders and expanding the foundation’s funding and community reach through advocacy and fundraising.

 **Primary Responsibilities:**

*1\.* *Leadership and Strategic Planning:*

* Develop and implement strategic initiatives to support firefighter families with a focus on mental health, family dynamics, and emotional resilience.
* Lead with empathy and insight into the specific challenges faced by firefighter families, ensuring that the foundation’s programs are responsive to their unique needs.
* Collaborate with the Board of Directors to establish long\-term goals and ensure alignment with the foundation’s mission.

*2\. Program Development and Oversight:*

* Oversee the development and delivery of programs and events aimed at improving the well\-being and mental health of firefighter families.
* Stay informed about emerging trends in psychological health, trauma, and stress management, especially those affecting first responder families.
* Partner with mental health professionals, social workers, and community organizations to offer effective services and support to firefighter families.

*3\. Fundraising and Resource Development:*

* Develop and implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy, including grant writing, individual donor cultivation, corporate partnerships, and events.
* Actively seek out and secure new funding opportunities to ensure the financial sustainability of the foundation.
* Build and nurture relationships with existing donors, sponsors, and potential philanthropic partners.

*4\. Community and Stakeholder Engagement:*

* Serve as the primary ambassador for the foundation, advocating for the mental health and well\-being needs of firefighter families at public events, media outlets, and industry forums.
* Cultivate relationships with fire departments, firefighter unions, community leaders, mental health professionals, and policymakers to advance the foundation’s mission.
* Engage firefighter families directly to better understand their needs and ensure the foundation’s services remain relevant and impactful.

*5\. Financial and Operational Management:*

* Manage the foundation’s budget and resources to ensure efficient and effective program delivery.
* Oversee all financial operations, including budget preparation, financial reporting, and compliance with nonprofit regulations.
* Develop and implement policies, procedures, and operational frameworks that align with best practices for nonprofit management.

*6\. Board and Governance Relations:*

* Serve as the key liaison between the Board of Directors and Council Members and the foundation’s operations.
* Collaborate with the board to ensure governance practices are in place, providing regular updates on the foundation’s progress, financial status, and strategic initiatives.
* Assist in the recruitment and orientation of new board of directors and council members as needed.

 **Leadership Responsibilities:**

* Regularly meeting with each team member to coach and mentor to ensure alignment around the organization’s mission, vision, and values.
* Providing team members with training, tools, and resources to successfully perform their jobs.
* Holding teams accountable for meeting all service level agreements, ensuring the team’s work is accurate/efficient, and providing coaching, feedback, or training as needed.
* Providing and ensuring development opportunities are available for team members as part of their overall career path.
* Leading change by identifying opportunities that consistently raise the bar and improve service levels.
* Assisting with executing the business plan of the foundation.

 **Basic Qualifications:**

* Education: Bachelor’s degree in Social Work, Psychology, Nonprofit Management, or related field
* Minimum of 5 years of leadership experience in a nonprofit, mental health, or first responder support organization.
* Deep understanding of the psychological and emotional challenges faced by firefighter families, such as trauma, PTSD, stress, and family dynamics.
* Proven experience in fundraising, with a successful track record in securing grants, sponsorships, and individual contributions.
* Strong leadership and team management skills, with the ability to inspire and motivate staff, volunteers, and partners.
* Excellent communication and public speaking skills, with the ability to represent the foundation in public forums and media outlets.
* Experience working with a Board of Directors and building governance structures for nonprofit organizations.
* Compassionate, empathetic, and mission\-driven with a passion for helping families i

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**About Cardea Health:**

Cardea Health is a non\-profit organization dedicated to providing compassionate health care to marginalized populations.


**Misson Statement:**

Our mission is to create and support programs that protect the health and autonomy of vulnerable individuals and promote equity and social justice to improve the well\-being of our entire community. Compassionate care for vulnerable populations.


**Workplace Culture:**

Cardea is dedicated to creating a workplace that celebrates diversity and actively seeks to include underrepresented communities. We believe that diversity drives innovation and fosters a more dynamic, inclusive, and productive work environment. We value unique perspectives, experiences, and talents, and we are committed to providing equitable opportunities for growth and advancement. Join us in building a team that reflects the rich diversity of our society and let's make a positive impact together.


**Our Cardea CARE(S) Values include:**

* Compassion
  

Big heart, steady presence
* Accountability
  

We own it: the work, the follow\-through, the impact
* Respect
  

We assume good intent and keep it real
* Engagement
  

We lean in and figure it out together
* Support
  

We’ve got each other across roles, teams, and sites


These values guide how we care for residents, collaborate with colleagues, and represent Cardea Health in the community.




**Primary Work Location:** Hybrid


This position is designated as Hybrid and combines remote work with onsite responsibilities.


Employees are expected to report to assigned Cardea Health locations, meetings, trainings, and events as necessary to perform essential job functions.


**Job Summary**

The Chief Operating Officer (COO) serves as a key member of Cardea Health's Executive Leadership Team and is responsible for providing strategic and operational leadership across the organization's administrative and operational functions. Reporting directly to the CEO, the COO ensures that the systems, infrastructure, resources, and operational processes necessary to support Cardea Health's mission are effective, compliant, scalable, and financially sustainable.


The COO oversees facilities and property management, food services, fleet operations, information technology, workforce scheduling, purchasing, operational compliance, administrative services, and enterprise\-wide operational initiatives. This leader partners closely with program, finance, and executive leaders to ensure operational excellence, resource stewardship, risk mitigation, and continuous improvement across all Cardea Health locations.


The COO translates organizational strategy into operational execution, builds high\-performing teams, develops key performance metrics, and creates systems that enable Cardea Health to deliver exceptional services to vulnerable populations.


The COO serves as the executive leader responsible for operational resilience, emergency preparedness, business continuity, and the systems necessary to ensure uninterrupted service delivery across Cardea Health's programs and facilities.


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**

**Executive Leadership \& Strategic Operations:**

* Serve as a member of the Executive Leadership Team and contribute to organizational strategy, growth planning, and operational decision\-making.
* Partner with the CEO and executive team to identify operational risks, opportunities, and priorities.
* Develop and implement organizational systems, policies, procedures, and performance measures that improve efficiency and accountability.
* Lead enterprise\-wide operational improvement initiatives and oversee execution of strategic projects.
* Establish operational dashboards and key performance indicators to monitor organizational performance.

**Risk Management, Business Continuity \& Emergency Preparedness:**

* Develop, implement, and maintain organizational business continuity and disaster recovery plans to ensure continuity of critical operations during emergencies, service disruptions, or other unforeseen events.
* Monitor organizational readiness related to life safety, environmental hazards, security incidents, technology outages, staffing shortages, and facility disruptions.
* Serve as a member of the organization's incident command structure and executive escalation team during emergencies, critical incidents, and operational crises.
* In partnership with program leadership, Compliance, People \& Culture, and Finance, ensure operational practices and procedures support regulatory compliance, contractual obligations, organizational policies, and risk mitigation efforts.
* Establish operational compliance monitoring, reporting, and corrective action processes to promote accountability, continuous improvement, and proactive issue resolution.
* Collaborate with executive leadership to develop and maintain organizational resilience strategies that support uninterrupted service delivery and mission fulfillment.

**Facilities \& Property Portfolio Management:**

* Provide executive oversight of all leased and owned properties across the organization.
* Oversee facility maintenance, capital repair projects, and long\-term infrastructure planning.
* Manage relationships with landlords, property owners, brokers, developers, and service providers.
* Direct contract negotiations and oversight for facility\-related vendors and service agreements.
* Develop and execute strategies related to property acquisition, disposition, lease management, and portfolio optimization.
* Ensure facilities operate safely, efficiently, and in compliance with applicable regulations.

**Food Services Operations:**

* Provide executive oversight of organizational food service programs.
* Monitor operational quality, regulatory compliance, and service delivery standards.
* Review financial performance, including revenue, expenses, and labor utilization.
* Collaborate with operational leaders to identify process imp

jobs.title: Director of Operations – Plaintiff Personal Injury Law Firm
jobs.description:
**Location:** Los Angeles, California  
**Employment Type:** Full\-Time  
**Work Arrangement:** In\-Office  
**Industry:** Plaintiff Personal Injury Law

**Position Overview**

Pyramid Legal is seeking an experienced **Director of Operations** to oversee the day\-to\-day operations of our growing plaintiff personal injury law firm.

This is a senior leadership position for someone who understands the lifecycle of a plaintiff personal injury case and has experience managing people, workflows, systems, and departmental performance. The Director of Operations will work directly with the firm’s managing partners to improve efficiency, implement standardized procedures, build specialized departments, monitor performance, and prepare the firm for continued growth.

The ideal candidate is not simply an office manager. We are seeking a hands\-on operational leader who can evaluate how work moves through the firm, identify bottlenecks, create accountability, and build systems that allow attorneys and case managers to focus on higher\-value legal and client\-facing work.

Key Responsibilities

Firm Operations and Leadership

* Oversee the firm’s daily non\-attorney operations.
* Manage and coordinate pre\-litigation departments and administrative staff.
* Develop clear reporting relationships, responsibilities, and performance expectations.
* Hold employees and department leads accountable for deadlines, quality, productivity, and client service.
* Serve as the primary operational liaison between the managing partners and staff.
* Identify operational problems and implement practical solutions.
* Help transition the firm from a generalist case\-management model to a specialized departmental structure.

Department Development

Help establish, manage, and improve departments that may include:

* Intake
* Case Opening
* Property Damage
* Medical Records and Billing
* Pre\-Litigation Case Management
* Demand Preparation
* Settlement Negotiations
* Litigation Support
* Client Experience
* Administrative Operations

The Director of Operations will define department responsibilities, develop handoff procedures, monitor workloads, and recommend when additional staffing is necessary.

Standard Operating Procedures

* Create, document, and implement standard operating procedures for recurring firm workflows.
* Develop checklists, templates, handoff procedures, and quality\-control standards.
* Ensure employees are trained on approved procedures.
* Review and update procedures as the firm grows.
* Reduce dependence on institutional knowledge and individual work styles.

Performance Management and KPIs

* Establish measurable key performance indicators for each department.
* Create weekly and monthly performance dashboards.
* Monitor case\-opening times, outstanding records, property damage backlogs, client communication, demand turnaround, case aging, and other operational metrics.
* Conduct regular department and employee performance reviews.
* Identify employees or departments that require training, process improvement, or additional support.
* Prepare operational reports for the managing partners.

Staffing, Hiring, and Training

* Evaluate staffing needs based on caseload, workload, and performance data.
* Assist with recruiting, interviewing, hiring, onboarding, and training.
* Create structured onboarding and 30\-, 60\-, and 90\-day training plans.
* Develop employee career paths and leadership opportunities.
* Work with the managing partners on compensation, accountability, and retention strategies.

Quality Control and Client Service

* Implement recurring file audits and quality\-control reviews.
* Ensure that files are complete, organized, and progressing through the case lifecycle.
* Monitor client communication standards and address service\-related concerns.
* Improve internal communication and department handoffs.
* Ensure operational problems are identified before they affect clients, deadlines, or case value.

Technology and Process Improvement

* Evaluate the firm’s case management, document management, phone, email, calendaring, and reporting systems.
* Improve the use of automation, templates, task workflows, and dashboards.
* Identify repetitive administrative work that can be streamlined or automated.
* Coordinate implementation of approved technology and process improvements.
* Ensure employees consistently use the firm’s systems and workflows.

Required Qualifications

* Significant experience working in a plaintiff personal injury law firm.
* Strong understanding of the pre\-litigation personal injury case lifecycle.
* Experience supervising case managers, legal assistants, intake staff, or other law firm personnel.
* Demonstrated experience building or improving workflows, systems, departments, or standard operating procedures.
* Strong leadership, communication, and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to hold employees accountable while maintaining a professional and positive workplace.
* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to analyze workloads, case inventories, performance data, and operational bottlenecks.
* Proficiency with law firm case management software, Microsoft Office, reporting systems, and workflow technology.
* Ability to work in person at the firm’s Los Angeles office.

Preferred Qualifications

* Seven or more years of plaintiff personal injury law firm experience.
* Prior experience as a Director of Operations, Firm Administrator, Practice Administrator, Legal Operations Manager, Chief Operating Officer, Senior Case Manager, Pre\-Litigation Manager, or Department Manager.
* Experience managing multiple departments or a substantial personal injury caseload.
* Experience helping a plaintiff law firm scale its operations.
* Experience implementing KPIs, dashboards, file audits, and performance\-management systems.
* Familiarity with automobile accident, premises liability, dog bite, UM/UIM, government claim, and other personal injury matters.
